2604.27928.title: Training-Free Tunnel Defect Inspection and Engineering Interpretation via Visual Recalibration and Entity Reconstruction
2604.27928.paper_summary: Tunnel inspection requires outputs that can support defect localization, measurement, severity grading, and engineering documentation. Existing training-free foundation-model pipelines usually stop at coarse open-vocabulary proposals, which are difficult to use directly in interference-heavy tunnel scenes. We propose a training-free framework TunnelMIND. Specifically, language-guided defect proposals are not treated as final outputs; instead, their spatial support is recalibrated at inference time through dense visual consistency, so that coarse semantic anchors can be transformed into more reliable prompts under tunnel-specific hard negatives. The resulting masks are further reconstructed into structured defect entities with category, location, geometry, severity, and context attributes, which are then mapped to retrieval-grounded explanation and engineering-readable report generation under expert knowledge constraints. On visible, GPR, and road defect tasks, TunnelMIND achieves F1 scores of 0.68, 0.78, and 0.72, respectively. Overall, TunnelMIND shows that training-free tunnel inspection can move beyond coarse localization toward structured defect evidence for engineering assessment.
2604.27928.paper_summary_zh: 隧道檢查需要能夠支持缺陷定位、測量、嚴重程度分級和工程文檔的輸出。現有的無需訓練的基礎模型管道通常停留在粗略的開放詞彙提議上，這在干擾較重的隧道場景中難以直接使用。我們提出了一個無需訓練的框架 TunnelMIND。具體而言，語言引導的缺陷提議並不被視為最終輸出；相反，它們的空間支持在推理時通過密集的視覺一致性進行重新校準，以便將粗略的語義錨點轉換為在隧道特定的困難負例下更可靠的提示。生成的掩碼進一步重建為結構化的缺陷實體，具有類別、位置、幾何形狀、嚴重程度和上下文屬性，然後在專家知識約束下映射到基於檢索的解釋和工程可讀報告生成。在可見、GPR 和道路缺陷任務中，TunnelMIND 分別達到了 0.68、0.78 和 0.72 的 F1 分數。總體而言，TunnelMIND 顯示無需訓練的隧道檢查可以超越粗略定位，朝向結構化的缺陷證據以進行工程評估。
2604.27928.author: Shipeng Liu et.al.
2604.27928.authors: Shipeng Liu, Liang Zhao, Dengfeng Chen, Zhanping Song
